channel.title: Health - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

1. Why do mosquitoes bite some people more than others? CWRU researchers explain  FOX 8 News
  2. WATCH — Don’t let the mosquitoes bug you. Here’s why they bite | story | Kids News  CBC
  3. VERIFY: Are mosquitoes more attracted to certain blood types? Here's what experts say  WCNC
  4. How mosquitoes really pick their victims at your next evening party  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  5. Here's why mosquitoes bite you more than others  Firstpost

item.title: Wrongful death lawsuit: Patient died after contracting Legionnaires’ disease at local hospital - WTAE
item.description:
1. Wrongful death lawsuit: Patient died after contracting Legionnaires’ disease at local hospital  WTAE
  2. Lawsuit: Patient died after contracting Legionnaires’ disease at UPMC Mercy hospital  TribLIVE.com
  3. Lawsuit alleges patient caught Legionnaires' disease at UPMC Mercy  USA Today
  4. Widow sues UPMC Mercy over husband's death, saying he contracted Legionnaires' disease at the hospital  CBS News

item.title: Could Young Stem Cells Be the Key to Healthier Aging? - SciTechDaily
item.description:
1. Could Young Stem Cells Be the Key to Healthier Aging?  SciTechDaily
  2. How we know that aging is a biological function, and why it matters  ScienceBlog.com
  3. Rejuvenation science: Rewinding biological clock works for mice, not yet for humans  Piedmont Exedra

item.title: What If A Few Breaths Of CO2 Could Help Fight Alzheimer’s? Research Sparks Hope - Dallas Express
item.description:
1. What If A Few Breaths Of CO2 Could Help Fight Alzheimer’s? Research Sparks Hope  Dallas Express
  2. Inhaling high-dose CO2 clears Alzheimer’s proteins from the brain  New Scientist
  3. Carbon Dioxide Reportedly Capable of Removing Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s Proteins  Samfiru Tumarkin LLP
  4. There May Be A New Way To Clear Alzheimer's Proteins From The Brain  HuffPost UK

item.title: Teens With ADHD Improvise Complex Pill 'Stacks' to Take With Their Adderall - Bloomberg.com
